title: Home - Industrial Engineering Summer Practice
content:
Industrial Engineering Summer Practice

Sessions held in IE03 for IE300 and IE04 for IE400. Meeting slides available in forms section. Students advised to review meeting slides and SP webpage carefully before emailing questions.

SGK insurance applications: The OCW system for SGK insurance applications is now active. Instructions located in General Information section require careful review before applying.

Summer internship contracts: Work contracts requiring signatures should be submitted to the Undergraduate Secretary Office (IE 129) with one-week turnaround time.

IE300 and IE400 Summer Practice Manuals available under Forms.

title: General Information - Industrial Engineering Summer Practice
content:
General Information

Summer internships must involve physical attendance at the workplace. Project-based internships (IE 400) require minimum 6-week duration and prior committee approval via detailed proposal submission.

Public Institution Internships:
All Turkish public institutions (Kamu kurumları) use a centralized application system. Further details available at the centralized career portal.

Erasmus Program Option:
Students may complete internships in Europe for 3-12 months through the Student Internship mobility program. Short-term completions (3 months) count as either IE300 or IE400, not both. Check the Erasmus office website for specifics.

SGK Insurance Application Process:

Timing Guidelines:
- Apply 2-3 weeks before internship start
- Leave one full week safety margin between application and internship beginning
- Avoid applying more than 2 months early or fewer than 5 days before start
- Applications process each Monday for internships beginning within following two weeks

Application Steps:
1. Login to OCW platform with student credentials
2. Complete SGK Insurance Application questionnaire with identity and company details
3. Upload Declaration Form regarding family health insurance
4. SGK documents become available on e-devlet 2-3 workdays before internship start

Emergency Process:
Complete excel form and email to committee (ie-staj@metu.edu.tr) with subject line noting student name and internship start date if Monday submission window unavailable.

Voluntary Internships:
Optional secondary internships receive SGK coverage up to one month per company, provided first internship's insurance is already issued. Foreign students cannot participate. Leave minimum one blank day between consecutive internships.

Paid Internship Procedures:
For private sector compensation:
- Complete Paid SP form questionnaire
- Download, sign, and upload Paid Summer Practice Form
- Submit bank receipt documenting payment received

title: Steps to Follow - Industrial Engineering Summer Practice
content:
Steps to Follow

Before Summer Practice:
1. Examine the Summer Practice Website early and thoroughly
2. Read the 'Summer Practice Manual' available under Documents/Forms tab to evaluate company suitability
3. Check information about paid insurance or school-paid SGK insurance
4. Secure a company through: checking departmental company list, networking, or reviewing previous year databases
5. Some companies require a document (letter) explaining the aim of the internship, minimum duration, and stating that the insurance will be paid by METU. Use the SP Application Letter template
6. Some companies need an SP protocol form (sözleşme). Submit completed forms to Undergraduate Secretary Office; retrieve signed copies within one week
7. Read insurance instructions carefully and apply for SGK insurance promptly
8. Begin your summer work

During Summer Practice:
1. Attend with full concentration, observe actively, and participate fully
2. Take regular notes for final report preparation
3. Prepare draft report while conducting practice (upload when semester begins)
4. Obtain completed and signed 'SP Evaluation Form' and 'Employer Survey' from supervisor
5. Supervisor may email forms to sp-belge@metu.edu.tr in PDF format (traditionally or digitally signed); postal submission also acceptable
6. If receiving payment: complete 'Paid SP Questionnaire' in IE300/400 course at OCW. Download, print, sign, and upload the scanned PDF form
7. Upload bank receipt (PDF) confirming payment under ODTUClass assignment

After Summer Practice:
1. Prepare report following Summer Practice Manual guidelines
2. Register for IE 300 or IE 400 during registration
3. Submit via ODTUClass by announced deadline (This is a strict deadline)
4. Report format: PDF only
5. Faculty will check reports for plagiarism and AI-generated content before evaluation

Evaluation Process:
1. Faculty members grade assigned reports
2. Reports requiring corrections returned in final month
3. Students receive 2-3 weeks for revisions
4. Submit corrected work via ODTUClass

Key Notes:
- Verify company meets departmental requirements
- Ensure professional report quality
- Complete requested corrections promptly
- Begin company search early

title: FAQ - Industrial Engineering Summer Practice
content:
Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How can I arrange a summer practice organization?
A: Two options exist: apply independently or await departmental announcements. The latter carries higher risk with limited capacity. Certain firms require the department to determine the candidates for summer practice.

Q: Which departments should I visit?
A: An all-around organizational tour is preferable, though you can still gather many observations through effective communications.

Q: What topics will I study?
A: Industrial engineering addresses systems run by people for the needs of others, in general. Students should connect coursework to practical experience.

Q: What are suitable bank divisions for IE 400?
A: Divisions must demonstrate integrated operation of people, financial resources, processes and information with performance expectations and ongoing decision-making functions.

Q: What is the required paperwork?
A: - Register IE 300/IE 400 at semester start
- Submit reports to Students Affairs Secretary (Room IE 128) within two weeks of following term
- Company submits evaluation form and employer survey
- Complete online questionnaire

Q: What constitutes a sufficient IE problem definition?
A: Problem identification should be the result of some methodical analysis supported by organization-specific facts.

Q: How do service vs. manufacturing approaches differ?
A: The critical issue is to see the analogies. Budget allocation parallels production planning; records substitute inventory.

Q: Can multiple students work at the same organization?
A: Each student submits separate reports. Problem definitions must differ; project parts may align for complex projects (maximum two students).

Q: Is there a service sector manual?
A: Available from departmental photocopy office.

Q: What about project-based IE 400?
A: Alternative to standard practice; requires minimum duration of 30 workdays (6 weeks). Two-page proposal submission required, must include company approval, submitted first week of practice.

Last Updated: 28/03/2024

title: IE 300 Summer Practice - 2025-2026 Introductory Session
content:
IE 300 Summer Practice - 2025-2026 Introductory Session

IE 300 is a mandatory (must course), non-credit course. Your grade must be S (Satisfactory) for graduation. It provides your first professional experience.

Prerequisites for IE 300:
- IE 102
- IE 251, IE 265, IE 241, OHS 101 (fall semester courses)
- One course from: IE 266 or IE 252 (spring semester course)
- ALL prerequisite courses must be completed with a grade of DD or higher. If any prerequisite is not completed with grade >= DD, IE 300 summer practice CANNOT be conducted.
- If you are on academic probation in the Fall semester, you cannot register for the IE 300 course even if you completed your summer practice.

When to Conduct IE 300:
- Conduct IE 300 in Summer 2025.
- Available time interval: June 25 – September 26, 2025 (approximately 3 months).
- Minimum practice duration: 20 workdays (4 weeks).
- Summer practice must NOT overlap with summer school if you are attending summer school courses.
- If you are in graduation status and have completed all coursework, you may conduct both IE 300 and IE 400 in succession during Summer 2025.

Registration and Report Deadline:
- After completing your summer practice, you must register for IE 300 in the immediate academic term: Fall 2025-2026.
- Summer practice reports are due within the first 4 weeks of the Fall 2025-2026 semester.
- Report due date: October 24, 2025.

Steps to Follow in IE 300:
1. Search for a manufacturing company (start as early as possible).
2. Evaluate the company suitability.
3. Apply to the company.
4. Apply for SGK insurance via METU OCW (2-3 weeks before start).
5. Conduct your summer practice.
6. Submit your report to the SP Committee.

Accepted Company Types for IE 300 (Manufacturing ONLY):
- Manufacturing firms: Automotive, Machine parts, Electronics, Furniture, Textiles, Consumer durables (refrigerator, washing machine, etc.)
- Batch process industries: Steel, Paper mills, Pharmaceutical, Food and beverages

NOT Accepted for IE 300:
- Continuous process industries: Cement, Concrete, Sugar, Flour mill
- Service industries: Hospitals, Hotels, Banking, Research organizations, Transportation, Cargo carriers
- Online or hybrid internships are NOT allowed. Must be 100% face-to-face in the factory.

Department SP Opportunities:
- The department lists companies for SP application on the SP website under 'SP Opportunities'.
- Check regularly for updates. Department either assigns you or you contact the company yourself.

SP Application Form:
- Some companies need an application letter explaining the internship aim, minimum duration, and that insurance is paid by METU.
- Download the SP Application Form from the SP website (Documents/Forms tab).

SP Protocol Form (Sözleşme):
- Some companies request a protocol/contract form.
- Submit the filled form to the Undergraduate Secretary Office and pick it up within a week.

Insurance (SGK):
- Apply for SGK insurance via METU OCW platform (https://ocw.metu.edu.tr) 2-3 weeks before your internship starts.
- The compulsory insurance covers: iş kazası ve meslek hastalığı sigortası (Law #5510).
- Insurance period: 20 workdays to 3 months.
- Insurance also covers internships abroad.
- If the company insures you directly, you do not apply to METU for insurance.
- Download and fill the 'Declaration Form for students with/without family health insurance' and upload it to OCW.
- Get your 'Sigortalı İşe Başlama Belgesi' from e-devlet before your internship starts.

During Summer Practice:
- Download the 'Evaluation Form and Employer Survey Form' (Başarı Belgesi + İşveren Anketi) from the SP website.
- Attach your photo to the first page.
- Company fills out and emails the forms to sp-belge@metu.edu.tr at the end of your practice.

Submitting Your Report:
- Upload your report (PDF format, docx converted to PDF) to ODTUClass IE 300 Turnitin assignment.
- Plagiarism check: similarity must be less than 20%.
- Report is based on the IE 300 Manual.
- Answer all questions in the manual with sufficient explanation and analysis.
- For questionnaire: use METU Survey as instructed by the SP Committee at semester start.

Paid Summer Practice:
- If you receive payment, fill out the 'Paid SP questionnaire' on OCW.
- Download, print, sign, and upload the Paid Summer Practice Form (İşsizlik Fonu Katkısı Bilgi Formu).
- Upload the bank receipt showing payment received.

Voluntary SP:
- You can do a voluntary internship for additional work experience.
- University provides insurance for voluntary SP if you have completed your second year.
- Voluntary SP insurance: maximum 1 month, for only one organization.

Candidate Engineering Programs:
- Candidate engineering activities during semesters do not count for IE 300 (SP cannot overlap with teaching period).
- If you continue candidate engineering during summer and work 20 full workdays consecutively, it can count for IE 300.

title: IE 400 Summer Practice - 2025-2026 Introductory Session
content:
IE 400 Summer Practice - 2025-2026 Introductory Session

IE 400 is a mandatory (must course), non-credit course. Your grade must be S (Satisfactory) in both report sections for graduation.

Prerequisites for IE 400:
- IE 300 (completed)
- IE 252, IE 323, IE 333
- Any TWO courses from: IE 304, IE 324, IE 372, IE 368
- ALL prerequisite courses must be completed with grade >= DD. If any prerequisite fails this requirement, IE 400 CANNOT be conducted.

When to Conduct IE 400:
- Conduct IE 400 in Summer 2025.
- Available time interval: June 25 – September 26, 2025.
- Minimum practice duration: 20 workdays (4 weeks). Duration can be longer if the company requests it.
- IE 400 can also be conducted during the winter semester break if 20 workdays are available.
- Summer practice must NOT overlap with summer school.
- If in graduation status and all coursework completed, you may conduct both IE 300 and IE 400 in succession during Summer 2025.

Registration and Report Deadline:
- Register for IE 400 in the immediate following academic term: Fall 2025-2026.
- Report due date: October 24, 2025 (within first 4 weeks of Fall 2025-2026).

Accepted Company Types for IE 400:
IE 400 accepts BOTH manufacturing AND service companies.
- Manufacturing firms: Automotive, Machine parts, Electronics, Furniture, Textiles, Consumer durables, Batch process industries (Steel, Paper mills, Pharmaceutical, Food and beverages), Continuous process industries (Cement, Concrete, Sugar, Flour mill)
- Service industries: Hospitals, Hotels, Banking, Research organizations (e.g., ODTÜ Teknokent), Transportation, Cargo carriers, Public institutions, NGOs

Work Arrangement:
- Manufacturing company: must be 100% face-to-face in the company/plant.
- Service company: can be face-to-face OR hybrid (face-to-face + online).

Conducting IE 400 - Options:
1. Individual: at least 20 workdays.
2. Group of two students: at least 20 workdays; each student writes their own report; the project/problem must be significantly more involved; notify the SP committee in the first week of practice.
3. Project-based: at least 6 weeks (30 workdays); requires a two-page proposal approved by the SP Committee BEFORE the internship starts; content and format differ from regular IE 400 (see manual).

Systems Design Project:
- During IE 400, you are required to search for a 'Systems Design Project' opportunity.
- Ask engineers about potential challenging problems for your SD project.
- Once identified, check with the SD Committee (NOT the SP Committee).

Submitting the IE 400 Report:
- Report has TWO sections: 'Questions' and 'Problem/Project', graded separately.
- Grade S is required in BOTH sections to pass IE 400.
- IE 400 has two types of manuals: (i) Manufacturing Manual and (ii) Service Manual.
- Upload report (PDF, docx converted to PDF) to ODTUClass IE 400 Turnitin assignment.
- Plagiarism check: similarity must be less than 20%.
- Your report is also checked for AI-generated content.
- For questionnaire: use METU Survey at semester start.

SP Opportunities for IE 400:
- Department lists companies on the SP website under SP Opportunities.
- Check regularly. Department either assigns you or you contact the company yourself.

Insurance:
- Same process as IE 300: apply via METU OCW 2 weeks before start.
- Compulsory insurance: iş kazası ve meslek hastalığı sigortası (Law #5510), period 20 workdays to 3 months.
- Voluntary SP insurance: max 1 month for one organization.
- Insurance also covers internships abroad.
- If the company insures you, you do not apply to METU.
